{{Quote|Recently disclosed documents and testimony from a captured Thalassian slaver on Gyndine have implicated four Outer Rim Senators as profiting from slaving ventures in their sectors. Senator Tikkes (Mon Calamari), Senator Bufus Ritsomas (Till Chorios), Senator Danry Ledwellow (Er'kit) and Senator Wuja Wojaine (Pydyr) were all implicated.|[[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] report|<ref name="HNN" />}}
'''Till Chorios''' was a celestial body<ref name="HNN" /> located in the [[Till Chorios system/Legends|Till Chorios system]],<ref name="EAOC" /> one of the [[Chorios systems]]<ref name="Chorios" /> situated in the [[Meridian sector/Legends|Meridian sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> By the [[Great ReSynchronization|date]] [[22 BBY/Legends|13:4:4]], [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Bufus Ritsomas]] represented Till Chorios in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]]. Ritsomas was implicated in participating in an Outer Rim [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] ring that saw the senator profit from the activities of [[Thalassian slavers/Legends|Thalassian slavers]] in the Meridian [[Sector/Legends|sector]].<ref name="HNN" />

==Behind the scenes==
Till Chorios was introduced in [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 50|''HoloNet News Vol. 531 #50'']], an issue of the [[HoloNet News (original website)|representation]] of the [[in-universe]] HoloNet News<ref name="HNN" /> that was written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and [[Paul Ens]]<ref name="Credits">{{HoloNetNewsWeb||credits.html|Credits}}</ref> and published by [[April 4]], [[2002]].<ref name="HNN50 date">{{JCFCite|url=posts/6308795|board=Literature|thread=The Official HoloNetNews Discussion Thread|user=Mavrick889|date=2002-04-04|time=3:16 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20220914072747/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-holonetnews-discussion-thread.5990764/page-8}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Till Chorios [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the celestial body itself, in grid square R-6.<ref name="TEA" />
